criteria.text: SKILL.md is substantially shorter and clearly acts as a loader/router.
references/core-design-principles.md is always required before DSL design or repair.
The new build and repair/iteration journeys are distinct and not mixed in user-facing behavior.
Capability files are loaded by condition, not always.
Failure-case files capture the real failures found in recent tests.
Existing validator checks still pass.
New regression tests cover the recent failures.
The Skill no longer treats local DSL sample folders as a runtime requirement.
User-facing text no longer exposes Grill with Docs, Gate, or Build Plan as process labels.
Generation before confirmation is blocked except for narrow repair.
User-facing text no longer asks users to choose normal mode or professional mode; depth is adapted internally.
The hard discovery checkpoint is expressed to users as one concise final alignment by default, while detailed brief and engineering records stay agent-facing.
Formal DSL projects produce a clean user-facing README.md plus versioned DSL file, with agent-facing project memory kept under .agent/.
Every old runtime reference is classified as migrated, rewritten, converted, or deprecated before it remains reachable from SKILL.md.
No legacy document remains in the active loading path if it conflicts with the vNext interaction model, output contract, validation rules, or no-local-runtime-dependency rule.
The legacy audit covers every runtime-influencing artifact: entrypoint, references, scripts, agent config, templates, validators, and indirectly referenced files.
A legacy audit manifest exists as implementation evidence, showing each old runtime-influencing artifact's decision, destination, removed conflict, and verification.

The legacy audit manifest is enforced as a loading whitelist: active Skill files can only load migrated or rewritten content, while converted and deprecated content cannot re-enter the main generation path.

Discovery has a sufficiency rule: once business goal, inputs, outputs, scenario, success standard, capability route, and key risks are clear enough, the Skill stops clarifying and moves to final alignment.
Non-trivial DSL designs show dimension-aware decomposition instead of placing unrelated planning, generation, validation, and packaging concerns into one overloaded LLM node.
Final alignment includes a plain-language dimension decomposition summary so users can understand the workflow design logic without reading implementation details.
Scenario-level regression tests cover vague new builds, clear documents, old DSL repair, known structural failures, final alignment behavior, and narrow repair exceptions.
Scenario tests are split into Interaction Regression and DSL Structural Regression, so user-facing behavior and generated DSL correctness are both verified.

Completion cannot be claimed until Legacy Audit Manifest check, Interaction Regression, DSL Structural Regression, and Spec Acceptance Audit have run or have manual evidence with explicit residual risks.
SKILL.md contains a short Non-Negotiable Rules section for entrypoint redlines, while detailed guidance remains in progressively loaded references.
Progressive disclosure is implemented as a hard ordered loading protocol: SKILL.md, redlines, core principles, journey, relevant capabilities, relevant failure cases, then validators/audits.
Reference files are one hop from SKILL.md in a flat references/ directory, using category prefixes for journey, capability, and failure-case files.
assets/fixtures/ is allowed only for tests and non-runtime examples; production DSL generation must not copy fixture files as templates.
